items.headline: Effects of Tirzepatide on Weight Loss and Chronic Inflammation in People With HIV
items.summary: This is a prospective cohort study of 12 overweight (with one or more weight-related condition) or obese adults with well controlled HIV-1 on antiretroviral therapy (ART). 5mg. Primary outcome: Change in Baseline Body Weight.

items.headline: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) Compared With Dulaglutide on Major Cardiovascular Events in Participants With Type 2 Diabetes

items.headline: Tirzepatide Titration to Reduce Side Effects in Individuals With Obesity
items.summary: The goal of this clinical trial is to determine whether a flexible, symptom-guided titration strategy for tirzepatide can reduce gastrointestinal side effects while maintaining weight-loss effectiveness in adults with obesity without diabetes. items.headline: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease, the HEpatic Response to Oral Glucose, and the Effect of Semaglutide (NAFLD HEROES)
items.summary: Background: In non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), fat accumulates in the liver and can cause damage. Researchers want to learn what causes the damage NAFLD, and to see if a medication can help. Primary outcome: Histological Improvement; Clinical Improvement; Change in hepatic gene expression.

items.headline: Young Adults With Early-onset Obesity Treated With Semaglutide
items.summary: Introduction: The increasing prevalence of obesity is particularly pronounced among adolescents. Currently available treatment options consist of structured lifestyle interventions. Primary outcome: Change in BMI (weight in kg/height in m^2).

items.headline: A Master Protocol of Multiple Agents in Adults With Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease (SYNERGY-Outcomes)
items.summary: The main purpose of the SYNERGY-OUTCOMES study is to find out whether retatrutide and tirzepatide can prevent major adverse liver outcomes (MALO) in people with high-risk metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD). Primary outcome: Time to First Occurrence of Any Component of the Composite Endpoint for Major Adverse Liver Outcomes (MALO).

items.headline: Effects of Tirzepatide in Obesity-Related HFpEF by Sex: A Prespecified Secondary Analysis From the SUMMIT Trial
items.summary: The SUMMIT trial showed that the long-acting glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide receptor and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist tirzepatide decreased risk of cardiovascular death or worsening heart failure (HF) in patients with obesity-related heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF).

items.headline: Real-World vs. Randomized Trial Evidence for Incretin-Based Therapies: A Narrative Review
items.summary: Incretin-based therapies have transformed the management of obesity and type 2 diabetes (T2D). Pivotal randomized controlled trials (RCTs) report substantial weight loss and glycemic improvements; the extent to which these translate to clinical populations remains uncertain.

items.headline: Rapid and harmonized analytical workflow for the determination of peptidic and non-peptidic doping agents in dried and liquid blood matrices
items.summary: Recently, methods for detecting small peptides in dried blood spots have been published. These procedures typically involve multiple sample preparation steps, resulting in labor-intensive and costly workflows.

items.headline: Efficacy and safety of incretin-based therapies in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus: a network meta-analysis based on clinical trials
items.summary: This study systematically assessed the efficacy and safety of 15 incretin-based therapies (IBTs) for type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM), including mono- (GLP-1RA), dual- (GIP/GLP-1RA or GLP-1/GCGR), and triple- (GIP/GLP-1/GCGR) receptor agonists, and explored how dosage and treatment duration affect clinical outcomes to support individualized treatment decisions.
